what_failed: The M1 exercise agent_loop.py looped 6x calling calculator('') and the self-test failed: the planner never registered that a tool had already been used.
why_it_failed: Root cause: a representation mismatch. run() stored each action as a formatted string `calculator('6 * 7')`, but think() checked `action == 'calculator'` (the bare name). The equality never matched, so the 'already done' guard never tripped. A second bug: the arithmetic regex captured a leading space instead of the expression.
signal_missed: The printed trajectory showed calculator('') repeating 6 times with an ERROR observation — a loud, visible signal that the guard wasn't working. Reading the trajectory (Day 4: the trajectory is the truth) located it in one pass; guessing would not have.

system_improvement.rule: When one function writes a value and another reads it, assert they share the same representation. Compare on a stable key (startswith bare tool name), not a formatted display string.
system_improvement.test_or_check: agent_loop.py --selftest now asserts BOTH tools were used AND the answer contains 42 AND sunset — catches the regression. scripts/check.sh O10 runs --selftest, so this can't silently rot.

guard.desc: M1 think() still compares on the bare-tool-name key (startswith), not the formatted action string

author: paul
